response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion:
As a retired 87-year-old man from Kansas City, I have seen the rise of social media and its impact on society. As someone who has lived through different eras, I understand that fake news and content can be detrimental to our mental health and well-being. Here are some tips on how to manage and deal with fake content on social media: 

1. Be selective of the sources you follow. Don't just follow anyone blindly, check their credibility and reputation before hitting that follow button. Look for sources that have a good track record of accuracy and fact-checking.
2. Use fact-checking websites and tools. There are many fact-checking websites and tools available online that can help you verify the authenticity of news articles and content. Some popular ones include Snopes, FactCheck.org, and PolitiFact.
3. Be cautious of sensational headlines. Many fake news articles have sensational headlines that are designed to grab your attention. Take a closer look at the article and check the source before sharing it with others.
4. Use social media platforms' built-in fact-checking tools. Many social media platforms, such as Facebook and Twitter, have built-in fact-checking tools that can help you identify fake content. Use these tools to verify the accuracy of the content before sharing it.
5. Educate yourself on media literacy. Media literacy is the ability to critically evaluate information and sources. Take the time to learn about media literacy and how to identify fake news and content. This will help you make informed decisions when consuming information online.

In conclusion, dealing with fake content on social media requires a combination of critical thinking, fact-checking, and media literacy. By following these tips, you can protect yourself from the harmful effects of fake news and content and ensure that your social media experience is safe and enjoyable.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: My opinion is based on my personal experiences and observations, as well as research into the impact of fake news on society. I have also consulted with experts in the field of media literacy and fact-checking to inform my opinions. Additionally, I have taken the time to educate myself on media literacy and how to identify fake news and content.
